name: Vervain Sacred
description: Vervain, particularly the species Verbena officinalis, holds a significant place in European folklore and mythology. Revered as a sacred herb, it was associated with protection, purification, and divination. The Celts and Druids considered vervain a potent magical plant, using it in rituals for cleansing sacred spaces, warding off evil spirits, and enhancing psychic abilities. Romans also prized vervain, calling it 'herba sacra' (sacred herb) and using it in religious ceremonies, treaties, and as an ingredient in love potions. It was believed to possess the power to heal wounds, cure ailments, and bring good fortune. In some traditions, vervain was linked to Christ's crucifixion, as it was said to have been used to staunch his wounds, further solidifying its sacred status. Throughout the Middle Ages, vervain continued to be employed in witchcraft and folk medicine. It was hung over doorways to protect against negativity, carried as an amulet for good luck, and used in spells for love, prosperity, and protection. The plant's association with diplomacy and peace is also notable, as it was sometimes used in peace offerings and negotiations. Vervain's enduring presence in European folklore highlights its perceived power and versatility as a magical and medicinal herb.
subtitle: Sacred herb of protection and magic

keyMyths.title: Vervain and the Druids
keyMyths.description: The Druids held vervain in high esteem, using it in sacred rituals and ceremonies. They believed it possessed the power to cleanse and purify, and to open pathways to the spirit world.

keyMyths.title: The Roman 'Herba Sacra'
keyMyths.description: Romans called vervain 'herba sacra' and used it in religious ceremonies and treaties. They believed it brought good fortune and healing.

sources.author: Pliny the Elder
sources.description: Ancient Roman encyclopedia containing information about vervain's medicinal and magical uses.
sources.text: Ancient Roman encyclopedia containing information about vervain's medicinal and magical uses.

sources.author: Cunningham, Scott
sources.description: Modern encyclopedia detailing the magical properties and uses of vervain in Wicca and other traditions.
